data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding attributable to this child, and NO uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED CRITERIA — FULLY covered by this child:
- Parent SC "Bidirectional round-trips (comments, links, parent) for the real Jira shapes are asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a AC1 (hermetic bidirectional round-trip of comments/links/parent through the production path, no live Jira) + AC2 (every NOT-SYNCED/"as expected" probe guard converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes / removed) + AC3 (hermetic round-trip in CI; documented opt-in live variant for the real-Jira directions). FULLY.
- Parent AC "Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip is asserted hermetically through the production path" → 822a AC1. FULLY.
- Parent AC "Every probe assertion that encodes a current bug as expected is converted to fail-when-the-gap-closes" → 822a AC2 (audit scripts/jira-pressure-test/*.sh for every NOT-SYNCED / "as expected" guard; convert to xfail/fail-when-the-gap-closes or remove; none silently passes on a defect). FULLY.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 822a AC4 (assertions check round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality); parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via the in-CI hermetic round-trip (AC3) staying green.

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings; this child's declared OUT item (producer↔consumer schema/contract → story B/8937) is consistent with the roster (and with 822a's declared depends_on fe57 for the fixtures/fake):
- AC SSOT snapshot-entry schema + AC fetcher-snapshot-validated-vs-schema + AC each-differ-validated-vs-schema + AC one round-trip contract test through the production path using real-Jira fixtures + SC producer/consumers cannot drift on snapshot shape → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (real-payload fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake shape fidelity).
- AC outbound collapsed to a single production path + AC retired outbound applier path deleted + SC outbound exercised through the production path, not a dead path → 33d0.
- AC outbound sub-op telemetry surfaced in the structured batch outcome + AC computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first) + SC silent sub-op no-op fails a test/canary → 2359.

ANTI-FP — the recurring "Parent AC6 (documented anti-change-detector guardrail) only PARTIALLY covered" finding from the earlier audits on THIS ticket is a FALSE POSITIVE and is WITHDRAWN. The actual parent acceptance criterion reads verbatim: "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)." It (a) explicitly NAMES its owner — story B = sibling 8937 — and (b) is FULLY covered there: 8937's description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact (resolves the roster-level gap): commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note recording the testing-strategy decision + the schema location + the rules) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered, not partial. The earlier "AC6 PARTIALLY" finding was raised against a paraphrased "governs new tests" wording that does not match the plan text and predates/ignored 8937's explicit ownership; it is not re-filed. (The most recent G3 audits on siblings 2359/33d0/59ef already withdrew the same false positive.)

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" + parent AC "hermetic bidirectional round-trip" + parent AC "every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded" (all OWNED by THIS child); CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" (822a AC4) + parent AC "suite green" (in-CI round-trip); COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip (8937+fe57+59ef), applier-collapse/retired-path-deleted (33d0), telemetry/canary (2359), and the documented guardrail (8937 AC5, explicitly named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent AC "all six closed; full reconciler suite green." UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.
